summary: Matched several player bonus-library functions in src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c, specifically pl_8003E4A4, pl_80040688, and pl_800407C8. The main work filled in pl_8003E4A4 using stale move table bookkeeping over mapped item/move kinds, adjusted a call condition in pl_80040688 to use the slot/local temp value instead of the second argument, and forced unsigned integer-to-float conversions in pl_800407C8 before averaging. The header prototype for pl_8003E4A4 was corrected from u8 slot to int slot to match the implementation and calling convention.
what_changed: Implemented pl_8003E4A4 in plbonuslib.c. The function obtains a pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t via Player_GetStaleMoveTableIndexPtr2(slot), scans an input move/item array, tracks seen mapped kinds in a local u32 seen[0x27] array, increments x7AC counters for seen entries, resets unseen entries, and triggers pl_80038788(slot, 0x9D, 1) when a counter reaches the threshold at pl_804D6470->x138.

Moved the inline helper match_item_kind earlier in plbonuslib.c so it can be used by the newly implemented pl_8003E4A4. The helper maps normal kinds 0..0x22 directly and remaps special kinds 0xCD, 0xE1, 0xE2, and 0x28 to indices 0x23..0x26, returning -1 for unsupported kinds.
Changed pl_80040688 so unk_cond is called as unk_cond(arg1, temp_r0) rather than unk_cond(arg1, arg2), which was part of making the function match.
Changed pl_800407C8 so xCEC and xCF0 are cast through u32 before being assigned to float temporaries and passed to pl_CalculateAverage, matching the expected unsigned conversion behavior.
Updated the pl_8003E4A4 declaration in plbonuslib.h from void pl_8003E4A4(u8 slot, bool, void*, int) to void pl_8003E4A4(int slot, bool, void*, int).
PR body states verification was run with python configure.py --require-protos && ninja.
smart_moves: The match for pl_8003E4A4 reused the existing match_item_kind mapping helper rather than duplicating item-kind normalization logic.
Using a fixed local seen[0x27] array mirrors the limited mapped item-kind range and lets the function separate deduplication of the input list from later counter updates.
The x7AC comparison casts the table counter to u32 before comparing against the global threshold pointer, preserving the signedness/codegen-sensitive behavior needed for matching.
The pl_800407C8 fix demonstrates a common matching tactic: explicit unsigned integer-to-float casts can be required even when the apparent source fields are numeric counters.
The header prototype was kept in sync with the matched implementation, and the PR verification used --require-protos, which helps catch stale or mismatched declarations.
decomp_lessons: For matched functions that take player slots, confirm the exact parameter width in both the C definition and header. Here pl_8003E4A4 required int slot rather than u8 slot.
When matching PowerPC integer-to-float behavior, signedness matters. Casting counter fields to u32 before assigning to float can be necessary to produce the intended conversion sequence.
If a helper is needed by an earlier function, moving the inline helper above its first use can avoid adding new declarations while preserving local helper scope.
Mapped enum/index spaces should be represented explicitly. match_item_kind documents that only 0x27 mapped indices are valid for the x7AC stale move table counters.
Small argument substitutions can be the difference between nonmatching and matching code. In pl_80040688, unk_cond needed the computed temp_r0 value rather than arg2.
naming_conventions: Unidentified matched functions retain address-style names such as pl_8003E4A4, pl_80040688, and pl_800407C8.
Local temporaries follow existing decomp naming such as temp_r0, temp_r0_2, temp_r3, temp_r5, temp_f31, and bits when semantic names are not yet established.
Known structures and fields use project-style names such as pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t, x0_staleMoveTable, x7AC, xCBC, xCC0, xCEC, and xCF0.
The helper name match_item_kind describes its purpose as mapping item kinds into the compact bonus/stale-table index range.
Header comments preserve address annotations, for example /* 03E4A4 */ before the pl_8003E4A4 prototype.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use explicit casts to u32 before float assignment to force unsigned conversion codegen, as in pl_800407C8 for xCEC and xCF0.
Use the same local variable in calls that the original assembly likely uses, as shown by changing unk_cond(arg1, arg2) to unk_cond(arg1, temp_r0) in pl_80040688.
Use fixed-size stack arrays and simple index loops for predictable codegen; pl_8003E4A4 zeroes and consumes seen[0x27] with explicit for loops.
Preserve comparison signedness with explicit casts, such as comparing (u32)table->x0_staleMoveTable.x7AC[i] against the u32 value loaded through pl_804D6470->x138.
Keep inline helper placement sensitive to call order; match_item_kind was placed before pl_8003E4A4 after that function began using it.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR body only listed matched functions and build verification; no reviewer rationale or requested changes were available.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs renaming pl_8003E4A4, pl_80040688, or pl_800407C8 to semantic names.
Search for additional uses or refinements of match_item_kind and the 0x27 item-kind mapping range.
Check whether pl_804D6470->x138 and x7AC receive semantic field names in later structure-cleanup PRs.
Search for other PRs that use explicit u32-to-float casts to match pl_CalculateAverage callers.
